---
label: "Query Rewriting & Retrieval Loop"
icon: "🔄"
---

## The Self-Correction Loop: Rewrite → Retrieve → Grade

When grading reveals poor retrieval quality, the agent doesn't give up — it rewrites the query and tries again. This is the "agentic" part of Agentic RAG.

### The Query Rewriting Node

<CodeBlock b64="" />

### The Routing Function

<CodeBlock b64="" />

### Vector Store Integration with ChromaDB

<CodeBlock b64="" />

<AnalogyBox emoji="🗄️" title="Vector Store = Semantic Matchcode">
If you've worked with SAP matchcode objects (search helps), a vector store serves the same purpose but for semantic meaning instead of exact field values. A matchcode finds "McKesson Corp" when you type "McKesson." A vector store finds documents about "pharmaceutical distribution challenges" when you search "drug supply chain problems." Both are indexes that speed up searching — they just operate on different dimensions.
</AnalogyBox>

<Quiz
  question="Your Agentic RAG system retrieves 5 documents but grading marks 4 as irrelevant. What should the routing function do?"
  options={["Generate anyway — 1 document is enough", "Immediately fail and return an error", "Rewrite the query and retry retrieval (if under retry limit)", "Remove the vector store and switch to web search"]}
  correctIndex={2}
  explanation="When most documents are irrelevant, the query likely needs refinement. The routing function should trigger a rewrite → retrieve → grade cycle. This is the core value of Agentic RAG — it self-corrects instead of generating a poor answer from irrelevant sources. The safety limit on retries prevents infinite loops."
/>

<SeeItInRe3 text="SnapSense uses the same retry pattern — if Open Food Facts returns no data for a barcode, it falls back to name-based search, then to UPCitemdb. Each fallback is a query rewrite targeting a different data source." />
